title: Post-op infection after knee surgery, no informed consent
body: Developed a serious staph infection at the surgical site within 48 hours of knee replacement. The surgeon never discussed infection risks during consent. Had to be readmitted for IV antibiotics and wound debridement. Considering malpractice claim.

title: Surgeon operated on wrong knee
body: Went in for right knee arthroscopy and woke up to find they operated on my LEFT knee. The correct knee still needs surgery. This is a textbook never event. My attorney says this is clear malpractice.

title: Anesthesia dosing error caused complications
body: During my knee surgery the anesthesiologist administered an incorrect dosage. I stopped breathing and had to be resuscitated. Spent 3 extra days in ICU. The hospital tried to cover it up in my medical records.

title: MRSA contracted during hospital stay
body: Tested negative for MRSA before admission. After knee replacement surgery I developed MRSA at the surgical site. The infection spread to the prosthetic and I needed a complete revision. 4 months of IV antibiotics.

title: Retained surgical sponge discovered 6 months later
body: Six months after knee surgery I was still in pain. CT scan revealed a surgical sponge left inside during the operation. Required a second surgery to remove it. The hospital hasn't even apologized.

title: Wrong medication administered, dosage 10x normal
body: Nurse administered 10x the prescribed dose of blood thinner post-surgery. I developed internal bleeding and needed emergency intervention. The medication error was documented in my chart but no one told me about it.

title: Fall from hospital bed — no side rails raised
body: Fell out of the hospital bed on my first night after surgery because the side rails were never raised. Fractured my wrist in the fall. I was on pain medication and sedated — this was clearly negligent.

title: HIPAA violation — records shared without consent
body: Discovered that my surgery records, including HIV status, were shared with my employer's insurance coordinator without my written consent. This is a clear HIPAA violation and I've filed a complaint with HHS.

title: Delayed diagnosis of compartment syndrome
body: After knee surgery I reported severe pain and swelling for 18 hours. Nursing staff dismissed it as normal. By the time they diagnosed compartment syndrome, permanent nerve damage had occurred. This delay was negligent.

title: Unnecessary joint replacement recommended
body: Got a second opinion and learned my knee doesn't need replacement at all. Physical therapy and injections would suffice. The first surgeon was pushing an unnecessary $45K surgery. This feels like billing fraud.

title: Prosthetic implant recalled by FDA post-surgery
body: Three months after my knee replacement I received an FDA recall notice for my exact implant model. The hospital never contacted me. Found out from a news article. Now facing potential revision surgery.

title: Billing fraud — procedures not performed
body: My insurance was billed for a nerve conduction study and ultrasound that were never performed during my knee surgery admission. When I questioned it, billing said it was a coding error. Reported to the state AG.

title: Physical therapy neglected, leading to stiffness
body: Was supposed to receive PT twice daily after knee replacement per protocol. Only got it 3 times in 5 days due to staffing shortages. Now I have permanent stiffness that may require manipulation under anesthesia.

title: Nerve damage from improper tourniquet use
body: Developed foot drop after knee surgery. Neurologist confirmed the peroneal nerve was damaged by a tourniquet left on too long during the procedure. The surgical report shows 3+ hours of tourniquet time.

title: DVT prophylaxis not administered post-op
body: Developed a blood clot in my calf 5 days after knee surgery. Review of my chart shows I was supposed to receive Lovenox injections and compression devices but they were never administered. Nearly had a pulmonary embolism.

title: Hospital-acquired pneumonia after extended stay
body: Caught pneumonia during my 6-day post-surgical hospital stay. I was immobile and the respiratory therapy that was ordered was never provided. Added 2 weeks to my recovery and significant medical bills.

title: Emergency response delayed — infection worsened
body: Called the nurse station at 2am with fever of 103 and chills. No one came for 45 minutes. By morning I was septic. The delay in starting antibiotics likely contributed to my longer hospitalization.

title: Consent form concerns
body: Looking back at my consent form, several risks that actually occurred were not listed. The surgeon rushed through the consent process 5 minutes before I was wheeled into the OR while I was already on pre-op sedation.

title: Good surgical outcome but communication was poor
body: The surgery itself went well and my knee feels great 6 months out. However, the surgeon spent maybe 3 minutes total with me across 4 visits. All questions were handled by PA. For $50K I expected more.

title: Excellent surgical team, smooth recovery
body: Dr. and the entire ortho team were outstanding. Surgery was on time, pain was well managed, PT started same day. At 3 months I'm walking without a cane. Highly recommend this practice.
